appellations.description: Wine appellations (officially recognized wine-producing regions) in California.

Appelation.description: Name of the wine appellation or county-level region. Represents a specific geographic area recognized for wine production. Type: TEXT. Examples: 'Sonoma Coast', 'Napa Valley', 'Chalk Hill', 'Mount Harlan', 'Calaveras County'.

Area.description: Broader wine-producing area or macro-region that the appellation belongs to. Type: TEXT. Examples: 'North Coast', 'Central Coast', 'Sierra Foothills', 'California', 'Central Valley', 'N/A'. Possible values include large geographic groupings.

County.description: County where the appellation is located. Type: TEXT. Examples: 'Sonoma', 'Napa', 'San Luis Obispo', 'Monterey', 'N/A'. Some appellations span multiple counties; 'N/A' when not applicable.

State.description: State where the appellation is located. Type: TEXT. All values in this dataset are 'California'.

isAVA.description: Whether the appellation is a recognized American Viticultural Area (AVA). Type: TEXT. Values: 'Yes' or 'No'.

wine.description: Individual wine entries with details on production, pricing, scoring, and grape varieties. Contains 500 records.

Appelation.description: The appellation (wine-producing region) where the wine was produced. References appellations.Appelation. Type: TEXT. Examples: 'Napa Valley', 'Sonoma Coast', 'Santa Lucia Highlands', 'Carneros'.

Cases.description: Number of cases produced for this wine. Type: TEXT (numeric string or NULL). Range observed: from small batches (e.g. '154') to large productions (e.g. '8000'). NULL if unknown.

Drink.description: Recommended drinking window or year. Type: TEXT. 'now' indicates the wine is ready to drink immediately; a year value (e.g. '2012', '2015') indicates the optimal future drinking year.

Grape.description: Grape variety used to produce the wine. Type: TEXT. Examples: 'Syrah', 'Chardonnay', 'Pinot Noir', 'Sauvignon Blanc', 'Zinfandel', 'Cabernet Sauvingnon', 'Merlot', 'Grenache', 'Malbec', 'Petite Sirah'.

Name.description: Name or label of the wine. May be the grape variety name, a vineyard name, or a proprietary brand name. Type: TEXT. Examples: 'Syrah', 'Quarzo', 'Chardonnay', 'Hyde Vineyards', 'Mesa Verde Vineyard', 'Appelation Series'.

Price.description: Retail price of the wine in US dollars. Type: TEXT (numeric string). Range: $9 to $225, average ~$45.

Score.description: Wine quality/evaluation score, typically on a 100-point scale. Type: TEXT (numeric string). Range observed: 78 to 98.

State.description: State where the wine was produced. Type: TEXT. All values in this dataset are 'California'.

Winery.description: Name of the winery that produced the wine. Type: TEXT. Examples: 'Chalk Hill', 'Favia', 'Mer Soleil', 'Fog Dog', 'Selene', 'Brander'.

Year.description: Vintage year of the wine. Type: TEXT (numeric string representing a year). Range observed: 2004 to 2009 (with some data anomalies up to 2066).
